mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year historical backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to yield positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 60% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent elevated inflation readings and restrictive policy rates create a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth correlates with negative 60-day returns in greater than 55% of quarterly observations over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150,000 signal robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which fuels consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll surges above the threshold fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging industrial production and unemployment trends indicate a broad economic contraction that reduces corporate cash flows, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The dual deterioration condition yields positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of historical triggers.
